title: Normal Forms: DNF, CNF and Sigma-Notation
summary: Explains how to derive Disjunctive Normal Form (DNF) and Conjunctive Normal Form (CNF) from truth tables, and introduces sigma-notation as a compact way to specify Boole functions.
content_points: DNF: for each row outputting 1, write a conjunction term containing each variable (negated if 0); OR all terms.
CNF is the dual: for each row outputting 0, write a disjunction term; AND all terms.
Sigma-notation Σ(m1,m2,...): lists minterm indices where the output is 1.
Number of variables needed: smallest n such that 2^n > max index in sigma list.
Minimisation goal: find a simpler equivalent expression via algebraic methods, Karnaugh, or Quine-McCluskey.
learning_objectives: Derive DNF and CNF from a truth table
Write a Boole function in sigma-notation
Explain algebraic minimisation using Boole laws
